meta.title: HEIC to JPG Converter | Free Online Batch Conversion Tool
meta.description: Free online tool for batch converting iPhone photos and multiple HEIC files to JPG / JPEG in your browser. No installation or sign-up required.

h1.title: HEIC to JPG Converter | Free Online Tool for Batch Conversion
h1.subtitle: Convert HEIC files to JPG / JPEG in batches. When you need to work through large batches of iPhone photos or multiple HEIC files, it reduces the repetitive work of adding, converting, and downloading files one by one. Free to use, with no installation or sign-up required, and you can start right in your browser.

intro.title: What Is HEIC JPG NOW?
intro.content: HEIC JPG NOW is an online HEIC to JPG converter built to make HEIC files easier to work with. Depending on the device, app, or service you use, HEIC files can be harder to open, share, or upload. When you need to convert multiple HEIC files, doing them one by one can also slow the whole task down. HEIC JPG NOW is designed to reduce that friction and make batch conversion easier to manage.

howTo.title: How to Batch Convert HEIC to JPG / JPEG

step1.title: Step 1: Add your HEIC files
step1.content: Use "Choose Files" or drag and drop to add HEIC files. If you want to batch convert, you can add multiple files at once or keep adding more with "Add Files."

step2.title: Step 2: Click Convert
step2.content: After selecting your files, click "Convert" to turn HEIC files into JPG / JPEG. A progress bar helps you see how the conversion is moving along.

step3.title: Step 3: Download the converted files
step3.content: Once conversion is complete, you can download files one by one with "Download" or get everything at once with "Download All." If you want to continue with another batch, click "Convert More."

features.title: Key Features of This HEIC to JPG Converter

free.title: Free to use
free.content: Start using the HEIC to JPG / JPEG conversion features for free right away.

batch.title: Batch convert multiple HEIC files
batch.content: Convert multiple HEIC files to JPG / JPEG in one go instead of handling them one by one.

browser.title: No installation required
browser.content: Use it directly in your browser without installing software or plug-ins.

devices.title: No sign-up required
devices.content: Add your HEIC files and start converting without creating an account or logging in.

privacy.title: Convert in your browser
privacy.content: The conversion runs in your browser, so you can process images without sending them to a server.

whyChoose.title: Why Choose This HEIC to JPG Converter?

stable.title: Start without splitting files into smaller batches first
stable.content: Even if you add multiple HEIC files at once, you can usually keep going without first breaking them into smaller batches.

continueAfterFailure.title: One failed file is less likely to stop the rest of the batch
continueAfterFailure.content: If one file cannot be converted, the rest of the files can usually keep converting.

zipNames.title: Files with the same name are less likely to go missing after download
zipNames.content: When you download everything together, files with the same name are saved separately, which helps avoid missing results caused by overwriting.

lessManualWork.title: So it is easier to carry the batch through to the end
lessManualWork.content: Taken together, these points make it easier to keep a multi-file job moving and get through conversion and download without breaking the flow.

useCases.title: Common Situations Where You May Need to Convert HEIC to JPG

devices.title: When you want to organize multiple HEIC files at once
devices.content: Useful when you want to turn multiple HEIC files into JPG at once, such as for travel photos, work assets, or organizing iPhone pictures.

social.title: When you need HEIC files to open more easily on Windows
social.content: If you need to open HEIC files on Windows, converting them to JPG first can make them easier to open. If you have a lot of files, getting them into the same format first also makes them easier to manage.

compatibility.title: When you want to share iPhone photos
compatibility.content: If you send HEIC photos taken on an iPhone as they are, the other person may not be able to open them depending on the device they use. Converting them to JPG first makes it easier to share multiple photos.

web.title: When you need to upload images to a website
web.content: Forms, CMS platforms, and some websites may not accept HEIC files directly. Converting them to JPG first can make it easier to prepare them for upload.

windows.title: When you want to send files to Android or other devices
windows.content: If you want to share files without worrying about device differences, converting them to JPG can make them easier to handle.

bulkTips.title: Tips for Batch Converting Large Numbers of HEIC Files
bulkTips.intro: When you batch convert a large number of HEIC files, matching the workload to your device and file sizes can help the process stay more stable.

desktop.title: A computer is usually better for larger batches
desktop.content: Compared with a phone, a computer is often better suited to moving through larger HEIC batches more smoothly.

time.title: More files can mean longer conversion and download times
time.content: As the number of files grows, conversion and ZIP downloads can take more time.

split.title: Process files in manageable batch sizes for your device
split.content: There is no fixed file-count limit, but if you are working with many files or larger file sizes, splitting them into manageable batches for your device can help the process stay more stable.

exif.title: If you need complete EXIF retention, a dedicated local tool may be a better fit
exif.content: This tool handles EXIF where possible, but if complete retention is essential, a dedicated local tool may be the better option.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions About HEIC to JPG Conversion

items.question: How do I convert HEIC / HEIF to JPG / JPEG on Windows 10/11?
items.answer: On Windows 10/11, you can open this tool in your browser, add your HEIC / HEIF files, convert them, and download the JPG / JPEG results. If you have a lot of files, using a computer is usually easier than using a phone.

items.question: Can I convert photos to JPG directly on my iPhone?
items.answer: Yes. HEIC photos taken on an iPhone can be converted to JPG / JPEG directly in your mobile browser.

items.question: Is it free? Do I need to install anything or sign up?
items.answer: It is free to use. No installation or sign-up is required, and you can start in your browser right away.

items.question: Is there a limit on how many files I can convert?
items.answer: There is no fixed file-count limit. For more stable use, we recommend staying within about 200 files per batch, 30 MB per file, and 1 GB total.

items.question: Why do you recommend about 200 files per batch?
items.answer: As the number of files increases, conversion and ZIP creation usually take longer. That is why we recommend about 200 files per batch, 30 MB per file, and 1 GB total as a practical guideline. If your files are larger or your device is slower, splitting them into smaller batches may work better.

items.question: What can cause a conversion to fail?
items.answer: Conversion can fail if a file is damaged, if you are processing many files at once, or if large files put too much load on the device. Differences in how HEIC / HEIF files were created or saved can also affect how easily they convert.

items.question: What happens if one file fails?
items.answer: If one file cannot be converted, the rest of the files can usually keep processing without forcing you to restart the whole batch.

items.question: Why do some HEIC files convert more easily than others?
items.answer: Even with the same HEIC / HEIF extension, files can differ slightly depending on where they came from and how they were saved. That is why some files convert more easily, while others are harder to process.

items.question: Will files with the same name overwrite each other?
items.answer: No. Even in a batch download, files with the same name are kept distinguishable.

items.question: Is EXIF preserved?
items.answer: This tool handles EXIF where possible, but complete retention is not guaranteed. If full EXIF retention is essential, a dedicated local tool may be a better fit.
